explanations.7: This checks if we're inside a git repository by trying to run a git command; the '>/dev/null 2>&1' part silently throws away any output so we only care whether the command succeeded or failed.
explanations.12: This checks if the repository has at least one commit yet, because some git commands fail on brand-new repos with no commits.
explanations.19: The '$\'e[1;36m' and '$\'e[0m' are ANSI escape codes that add color and formatting to terminal text—this makes the header cyan and bold.
explanations.23: The '%(if:equals=...)%(then)...%(else)...%(end)' is a conditional formatting syntax in git that checks if the branch name matches the current branch, then colors it differently.
explanations.30: This removes any shell alias named 'gst' if it exists, so the function you're about to define won't be shadowed by an older command.
explanations.38: This is a special Zsh hook function that automatically runs every time you change directories, making the git info display update whenever you 'cd' around.
